free_text: Proton-induced spallation at 600 MeV
- same data; additional
uncertainty in proton flux was added from this ref.
An independent experiment of 10Be and 26Al production
at 600 MeV.
HETC/KFA-2 CODE.
Total uncertainty excluding uncertainty in
proton flux
The uncertainty of target atoms in the beam and
their homogeneity over the target area is 2% and
impurities in the target materials, fluctuations in
beam intensity, interference by reactions of secondary
particles, recoil loss and recoil contamination,
reproducibility of photopeak integration,
statistical counting error-this source of uncertainty
is included by calculation the errors of individual
photopeaks. The error in the background subtraction was
taken into account.
Error in the monitor cross section.
Error in the absolute calibration of gamma-ray
spectrometers.
Error in the dead time and pile-up loss in
gamma-ray spectrometry
Relative efficiency calibration of of gamma-ray
spectrometers.
The uncertainty in proton flux (6%) is excluded
in the compiled uncertainty
Corrected to 4-dig. year and lower cases.
Correction according Memo CP-D/805 and INDC(GER)-0053.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
High-purity quartz discs of suprasil quality were used.
